title: Sous-vide Garlicky Brazilian Stir-fry
description: This lazy Sunday dish features a creamy and garlicky stir-fry of green peppers with a sous-vide preparation that keeps the fat content low.
instructions: Trim the stems from the green peppers. Cut into thin strips.
In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add sliced onions and chopped garlic; sauté for about 2 minutes until onions are translucent.
Add the pepper strips to the pot with the onions and garlic. Stir in soy sauce, chicken broth or water, salt, pepper, and optional thyme sprigs.
Bring to a simmer and let cook for about 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the peppers are tender and the mixture is creamy.
Transfer the cooked mixture into a sous-vide bag. Cook in your sous-vide machine preheated at 145°F (63°C) for 2 hours.
Let the prepared dish rest for about 10 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
